title: Cyrtochilum refractum
intro: Cyrtochilum refractum is a species of orchid native to Central and South America, characterized by its unique, fragrant flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health issues such as wound healing and infections. Cyrtochilum refractum contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Cyrtochilum refractum include tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which are often used topically to take advantage of the plant's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
taxonomy: Cyrtochilum refractum is a species of orchid in the subtribe Cyrtochiliinae. This subtribe is a part of the orchid family (Orchidaceae) and is native to the Americas. Cyrtochilum refractum is classified under the family Orchidaceae, subtribe Cyrtochiliinae, tribe Cranichideae, and family Asparagaceae subfamily Orchidoideae. The classification of Cyrtochilum refractum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. Further classification details may be found in the International Plant Names Index or other botanical resources.
